topic: Prepositions Part 2 — Direction & Movement (To, From, Into, Onto, Through, Across, Along)

sections.title: Prepositions Part 2 — Direction & Movement (To, From, Into, Onto, Through, Across, Along) — Introduction and Formula
sections.hindiTitle: Prepositions Part 2 — Direction & Movement (To, From, Into, Onto, Through, Across, Along) — परिचय और फॉर्मूला
sections.explanation: Prepositions Part 2 — Direction & Movement (To, From, Into, Onto, Through, Across, Along) is an essential building block of English grammar. Formula: Preposition + noun/pronoun to show direction or movement. Rules: 'To' shows destination, 'From' shows origin | 'Into' shows entering, 'Onto' shows moving on top of | 'Through' means passing inside something, 'Across' means from one side to another, 'Along' means moving beside something | Common mistake: using "in" instead of "into" for movement
sections.formula: Preposition + noun/pronoun to show direction or movement

examples.english: The cat went into the room.

examples.hindi: हम जंगल के बीच से गुजरे।
वह सड़क के उस पार गया।

sections.formula: Preposition + noun/pronoun to show direction or movement

examples.hindi: मैं ऑफिस से घर जा रहा हूँ।
examples.english: I am going home from the office.

examples.hindi: वह पुल के उस पार गई।

sections.explanation: How to make negative sentences and questions using Prepositions Part 2 — Direction & Movement (To, From, Into, Onto, Through, Across, Along). Common mistakes are highlighted here.
sections.formula: Preposition + noun/pronoun to show direction or movement

examples.hindi: हवा खिड़की से आ रही है।
examples.english: The air is coming through the window.

examples.hindi: हम मैदान के उस पार गए।
यह ट्रेन दिल्ली से मुंबई जाती है।
